title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Gastritis
intro: Herbal teas for Gastritis are a natural and effective remedy used to soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms associated with gastritis. This type of tea is made from herbs and plants that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to calm the digestive system and promote healing. Examples of herbal teas used to treat gastritis include peppermint tea, which helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce nausea; ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach; chamomile tea, which calms the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate gastritis symptoms; licorice root tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties and soothes the stomach lining; and dandelion tea, which helps to stimulate digestion and promote the healing of the stomach lining. By incorporating these herbal teas into one's diet, individuals can experience relief from gastritis symptoms and promote overall digestive health.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale teas, particularly the presence of gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including gingerol, shogaol, and zingerone, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, contributing to their therapeutic effects in treating gastritis. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat gastritis include reduced inflammation, alleviated symptoms, and improved overall digestive health.
preparation_procedure: Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or pot.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root.
Add the measured root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 cup, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BioFactors (Oxford, England)", Zingiber officinale teas for gastritis may be beneficial due to the anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative functions of ginger, specifically the phenolic compounds called gingerols, which can inhibit various pro-inflammatory cytokines and pathways involved in inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be beneficial in treating gastritis due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and be cautious when combining it with other medications or herbal preparations that may interact with it.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoon of dried ginger root for each cup.
Start with small doses (1/2 teaspoon) if you're new to ginger.
Take the tea 30 minutes before meals to help with digestion.
Continue usage for 7-14 days to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the root of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these teas help to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation, promoting healing and relief from symptoms. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, play a crucial role in treating gastritis by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat gastritis include reduced symptoms, improved digestion, and a faster recovery rate, making them a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the root.
Add honey to taste if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for gastritis relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas may have potential anti-ulcer activity and could be used to help prevent or cure gastritis caused by various factors, including inflammation and irritation in the mucosal lining.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can provide relief from gastritis symptoms, but using them for an extended period may lead to side effects such as high blood pressure, edema, and potassium deficiencies due to the licorice root's ability to disrupt the body's electrolyte balance. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to consume Glycyrrhiza glabra teas in moderation, ideally for short periods, and to monitor your blood pressure and potassium levels closely.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons if symptoms persist.
Consume tea for 2-4 weeks for best results.
Consult doctor for dosage adjustments if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, an inflammation of the stomach lining, due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat gastritis by reducing inflammation, calming the digestive system, and relieving symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain. The bioactive constituents of Foeniculum vulgare, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils, particularly anethole, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By consuming Foeniculum vulgare teas, individuals can experience relief from gastritis symptoms and promote overall digestive well-being.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers. You can use 2-3 sprigs or 2-3 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Add the Foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers to the boiling water. If using dried leaves, add 2-3 tablespoons.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, after meals, to help soothe gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Foeniculum vulgare teas can be used to alleviate gastritis symptoms; however, potential side effects may include allergic reactions, dizziness, and interactions with other medications, such as antacids or blood thinners, which could exacerbate the condition. To safely use Foeniculum vulgare teas for gastritis treatment, it is essential to start with small doses, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid consuming it for extended periods, as prolonged use may lead to gastrointestinal issues like diarrhea or stomach upset.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Foeniculum vulgare tea for gastritis.
Drink 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried Foeniculum vulgare per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue for 2-4 weeks, or as directed by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, a condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory properties of Cinchona officinalis teas, including the presence of alkaloids such as quinine, help to reduce inflammation and soothe the stomach lining. These bioactive constituents also exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help to eliminate the underlying cause of gastritis, such as bacterial or viral infections. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat gastritis include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and a decrease in symptoms such as abdominal pain and nausea.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of water and 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark. Use a clean pot and scoop.
Boil the water in the pot, then add the Cinchona bark. Turn down the heat to low.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea to sweeten it. Stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat gastritis, but potential side effects may include nausea, stomach cramps, and dizziness, particularly when consumed in excess or without proper guidance. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, it is essential to start with low doses, gradually increase the amount as needed, and avoid combining it with other stimulants or medications that can exacerbate stomach issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Cinchona officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried herb per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase to 1.5 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Take Cinchona officinalis tea for 2-4 weeks to manage gastritis.
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage advice.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa teas, including curcumin and polyphenols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and pain associated with gastritis. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa teas can help to alleviate symptoms of gastritis, such as heartburn, nausea, and abdominal pain. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat gastritis include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced overall gut health, making it a popular natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the boiling water to make it sweet.
Pour the boiling water into a cup, then add the Curcuma longa powder.
Stir the mixture well for 10 seconds to mix the powder with the water.
Drink the tea immediately, and repeat 2-3 times a day for gastritis relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Open life sciences", Curcuma longa teas for gastritis contain curcumin as their main active substance, which has potential in preventing or treating digestive diseases due to its mechanisms of action.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be beneficial in treating gastritis, however, possible side effects may include digestive issues such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its spicy and warming nature. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and drink plenty of water to help alleviate digestive discomfort.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Curcuma longa tea for gastritis treatment.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa root powder per cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea, 2-3 times a day, after meals and before bedtime.
Start with low dose (1 cup) for 1 week and increase to 2 cups if needed.
Continue using Curcuma longa tea for 2-3 months to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Mentha x piperita teas, also known as peppermint tea, have been widely used to treat gastritis, an inflammation of the stomach lining. The bioactive constituents of peppermint tea, including menthol and menthone, have been shown to have gastroprotective effects, reducing acid production and improving digestion. Heat the water in a pot or kettle until it boils.

Pour the boiling water over the peppermint leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM", Mentha x piperita teas for gastritis show gastroprotective activity due to its antioxidant properties and may be effective in reducing symptoms of gastritis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat gastritis, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Start with 1 teaspoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, an inflammatory condition of the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory and soothing properties of this herbal preparation help to calm the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastritis. preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe barbadensis gel. Scoop it from the leaf. Discard the yellow sap.
Wash the gel in a fine-mesh strainer under cold running water. Rinse well.
Combine 1 cup of water and the Aloe gel in a saucepan. Heat on low until warm.
Let the tea mixture steep for 10-15 minutes. Strain it into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer.
Drink 1 cup of the Aloe tea 2-3 times a day. Store leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Aloe barbadensis teas for gastritis have potential due to the presence of active substances such as flavonoids and saponins, which may help alleviate pain and discomfort in patients.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can have potential side effects when used to treat gastritis, such as stomach cramps, diarrhea, and nausea, especially when consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a small dose, gradually increasing as needed, and to avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, as this may exacerbate digestive issues.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis tea 2-3 times a day
Take 2-3 tablespoons of gel from the aloe plant 2-3 times a day
Use aloe gel for 2-3 weeks to see noticeable improvements

Consult a doctor before using aloe for gastritis treatment

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat gastritis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat gastritis by reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining from acid damage, thereby alleviating symptoms such as nausea and abdominal pain. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, including flavonoids, polyphenols, and catechins, work together to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promote healing of the stomach lining. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat gastritis include reduced symptoms, improved digestion, and enhanced overall gut health, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis (green, white, or oolong) tea leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water to 160-170°F (71-77°C) for green tea, or 180-190°F (82-88°C) for white or oolong tea.
Measure 1 teaspoon of loose tea leaves (or 1 tea bag) for every 1 cup of water.
Steep the tea for 1-3 minutes, depending on the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea and serve it without sugar or milk to help alleviate gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can help alleviate symptoms of gastritis, but using it to treat this ailment may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, particularly if consumed in excess or with other irritants. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to drink Camellia sinensis teas in moderation, avoid combining it with other herbs or spices that may exacerbate stomach issues, and take breaks from consumption to allow the stomach to rest and recover.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, an inflammatory condition of the stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties of thyme help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections that can exacerbate gastritis. The bioactive constituents of thyme, including thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that help to soothe and protect the stomach lining, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of gastritis. Regular consumption of thyme tea has been found to provide relief from gastritis symptoms, such as bloating, nausea, and abdominal pain, by reducing inflammation and promoting the repair of the stomach lining.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, or 1 teaspoon of dried Thymus vulgaris.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Add the fresh or dried Thymus vulgaris to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the Thymus vulgaris.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help soothe gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to PloS one, Thymus vulgaris teas for gastritis may be beneficial as they contain a total plant extract that has shown significant gastroprotective potential, including decreased ulcer area, increased mucus secretion, and higher pH of gastric content, as well as protection against stomach epithelium damage and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be beneficial in treating gastritis, but it may cause side effects such as increased heart rate, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals. When using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat gastritis, it is recommended to take precautions, such as starting with a low dose and gradually increasing it, to avoid interactions with other medications and to monitor for signs of allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, an inflammation of the stomach lining, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents present in Echinacea purpurea teas, such as alkylamides, caffeic acid, and phenolic acids, help to soothe the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. By modulating the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea teas can help to alleviate gastritis symptoms, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat gastritis include reducing the risk of complications, promoting faster healing, and providing relief from painful symptoms, making it a valuable herbal remedy for managing gastritis.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea roots and flowers.
Steep 1 cup of boiling water in a cup and let it cool for 1 minute.
Combine the Echinacea purpurea and the cooled water in a cup.
Let the Echinacea purpurea steep in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day for gastritis relief.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be beneficial in treating gastritis, but possible side effects may include nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its potential to irritate the digestive system. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to consume Echinacea purpurea teas in moderation, avoid taking it on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response to the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used to treat gastritis, a chronic inflammation of the stomach lining, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents, such as paeoniflorin and paeonol, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gastritis by modulating the immune response and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Paeonia lactiflora teas can help to treat gastritis and alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting. The benefits of using Paeonia lactiflora teas to treat gastritis include reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and a faster recovery time, making it a promising natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Paeonia lactiflora root. If using fresh, clean and dry it.
Cut 2-3 tablespoons of the root into small pieces. You can use a coffee grinder to make it fine.
Combine the root pieces with 1 cup of water in a saucepan. Bring the water to a boil.
Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help ease gastritis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used to treat gastritis, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals, especially when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. Precautions should be taken when using Paeonia lactiflora teas, such as starting with small doses and gradually increasing as needed, avoiding consumption before bedtime, and being cautious when combining with other medications or substances that may interact with it.

preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for gastritis have potential in alleviating pain and discomfort in patients due to the presence of phytochemicals such as curcumin, phenolics, and flavonoids.
intro_study: According to the study, teas for gastritis, specifically green tea consumption, may effectively improve diarrhea and reduce hospital stay in children suffering from viral gastroenteritis.
